name: The Eumelidai

leader.name: Lysandros the Elder
personality_notes: Venerable, immovable; sees every change as decay.

blurb: "the well-flocked," old wealth in land and herds
description: The senior clan: vast estates, an ancient name, treats the city as theirs by right.

name: The Pyrrhidai

leader.name: Kleon Pyrrhos
personality_notes: Young, brilliant, impatient for primacy.

description: A rising house, new money married into old blood, hungry to displace the Eumelidai.

name: The Skiadai

leader.name: Demarete of the Skiadai
personality_notes: Gracious in public, ruthless in the dark.

description: A once-great house in decline, clinging on through favors, debts, and quiet leverage.

name: The Tidesworn

leader.name: Nereus Halios
personality_notes: Booming and volatile; speaks of the god's anger as if it were his own.

description: The sea-god's cult — storms, earthquakes, the harbor's fear. They claim the god's wrath as leverage.

name: The Greenmantle

leader.name: Chloris of the Furrow
personality_notes: Warm and patient; thinks in seasons, slow to anger and slow to forgive.

blurb: the harvest cult of the earth-mother (Demeter)
description: The grain-and-fertility cult, rooted in the seasons; blesses the fields and feeds the festivals.

name: The Raving Choir

leader.name: Eurymache the Raving
personality_notes: Wild-eyed, speaks in the god's tongue; no one knows what she'll do next.

blurb: ecstatic prophets of the mad god (Dionysos)
description: Frenzied seers who prophesy in fits and whip crowds into ecstasy. Beloved, feared, uncontrollable.

name: The Bright Order

leader.name: High Seer Aletheian
personality_notes: Calm and smiling; every prophecy is a move on a longer board.

blurb: the great oracle of the far-shooting god (Apollo)
description: The establishment oracle — prestigious, panhellenic, consulted before wars and colonies. Kingmakers in robes.

name: The Shieldsworn

leader.name: Drakon the Steadfast
personality_notes: Stolid, dutiful; distrusts hired blades and glory-hounds alike.

description: The hoplite militia of property-owning citizens, the city's backbone — and proud that only citizens should bear its spear.

name: The Oarsworn

leader.name: Naukrates of the Long Oars
personality_notes: Bold and restless; dreams of a city whose walls are its ships.

blurb: the war fleet and its crews
description: The triremes and the citizen-rowers who drive them — the city's reach across the sea, and a restless power base of the common man.

name: The Netmenders

leader.name: Glaukos the Weathered
personality_notes: Salt-cured and plain-spoken; trusts the tide more than any magistrate.

blurb: the fishing crews who feed the city
description: The boat crews who work the waters — weather-beaten, independent, traditional. The city's daily catch and a food supply unto themselves.

name: The Storehouse Ring

leader.name: Hieron the Patient
personality_notes: Soft-spoken and predatory; waits for famine the way others wait for harvest.

blurb: the warehouse speculators who corner the market
description: A cartel that buys up stock and sits on it — hoarding grain and goods to choke supply and spike the price, then selling at the city's worst hour.

name: The Bronzehands

leader.name: Akmon the Forgemaster
personality_notes: Soot-stained and blunt; guards the secrets of the forge like family.

description: The forges — bronze and iron, arms, tools, fittings. Their hammers arm the city and shoe its every need.

name: The Keelwrights

leader.name: Argeios the Keelwright
personality_notes: Steady and far-thinking; every keel he lays is a bet on a bigger fleet.

description: The yards that build the hulls — merchantmen and triremes alike. They rise and fall with the city's ambitions on the water.

name: The Kerameis

leader.name: Pamphilos of the Kiln
personality_notes: Particular and vain; signs his finest work and remembers every slight.

blurb: the potters and vase-painters of the kiln-quarter
description: The clay-workers — everyday vessels and prized export ware. Numerous, ink-fingered, and prouder of their art than their station.

name: The Ovenmen

leader.name: Mazon the Baker
personality_notes: Flour-dusted and genial; smiles wider as the price of bread climbs.

description: The mills and ovens that turn grain into flour and flour into bread — the city's daily loaf, and a quiet lever whenever the granaries run low.

name: The Winepressers

leader.name: Oinops the Vintner
personality_notes: Ruddy and convivial; generous with a cup, shrewd over a ledger.

description: The presses and cellars behind the city's wine — from the rough stuff of the docks to the vintages of noble tables. Wine is everywhere, and so are they.

name: The Oil-pressers

leader.name: Elaios of the Press
personality_notes: Broad and unhurried; thinks in harvests and amphorae shipped abroad.

description: The presses that yield the city's olive oil — food, lamplight, athletes' sheen, and its single greatest export. Wealthy, and they know it.

name: The Tanners

leader.name: Bursas the Tanner
personality_notes: Blunt and foul-handed; a commoner's voice with a commoner's grudges.

description: The reeking tan-yards at the edge of town — leather for armor, sandals, harness, and rigging. Rich, coarse, and loud in the assembly.

name: The Weavers

leader.name: Theano of the Loom
personality_notes: Soft-spoken and tireless; weaves alliances as readily as cloth.

description: The looms that clothe the city — wool, linen, and dyed cloth for home and trade. Patient and domestic, and more powerful than their quiet suggests.

name: The Asklepiads

leader.name: Akesias the Physician
personality_notes: Calm and exacting; collects a quiet favor with every patient he saves.

description: The secular doctors who trade in the one thing no one can refuse — health. Indispensable, and they know the worth of a cure withheld.

name: The Players

leader.name: Komos of the Stage
personality_notes: Vain and magnetic; plays the audience like a lyre and never forgets a critic.

blurb: the actors, musicians, and poets of the stage
description: The theatre folk who command the crowd's heart — beloved, mocking, and able to make or unmake a reputation in an afternoon's performance.

name: The Quillsworn

leader.name: Grapheus the Reckoner
personality_notes: Meticulous and unhurried; forgets nothing, files everything, and counts twice.

blurb: the city's clerks, record-keepers, and tax-farmers
description: The bureaucrats who run the paperwork and the purse — registries, deeds, proclamations, and the collection of the city's taxes, which they bid to farm and quietly skim. They know where every document and every drachma sits.

name: The Perfumers

leader.name: Myrrha of the Flask
personality_notes: Elegant and discreet; knows what the great families whisper, and to whom it's worth a word.

description: Blenders of myron for the rich and the temples — but their real trade is access: scent, flattery, and the secrets of every household they serve.

name: The Elaiades

leader.name: Theron of the Slopes
personality_notes: A younger house, oil-rich and hungry for an old name.

blurb: the olive-groves of the inland slopes
description: a younger branch of the old estates, rich in oil if not yet in name.

name: The Builders

leader.name: Mnesikles the Master
personality_notes: Stone and timber speak with one voice; conflict is wasted labour.

description: the joined guild of stone and timber that raises wall, roof, and temple.

name: The Dockhands

leader.name: Glaukos of the Quay
personality_notes: Nothing moves through the harbour but by their leave.

description: labour and authority of the quay - berths, customs, and how much the harbour can bear.

name: The Merchant Houses

leader.name: Demaratos the Elder
personality_notes: The grain and the gold both flow through their ledgers.

description: the great houses that move grain and oil by sea - the city's lifeline and its purse.

name: The City Guard

leader.name: Captain Brasidas
personality_notes: Order within the walls, by the spear if the bread fails.

description: the force that keeps the peace at home - and leans on the crowd when it must.
